extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco demolished the 70km opening time trial, winning by 10 seconds over GANNA and gaining 43 seconds on GC rival ROGLIČ
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL gained 20 seconds at first intermediate check on 70km TT, signaling he peaked early rather than pacing conservatively.
The 2km final climb to Ortona amplified time gaps disproportionately—EVENEPOEL gained half his advantage on GANNA there alone.
UAE Emirates deployed dual TT threats VINE and MCNULTY early, using team depth to pressure later starters psychologically.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: 20-second lead at first check on 70km TT reveals he can sustain maximum power output far longer than traditional TT pacing models suggest
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: losing 43 seconds to EVENEPOEL on opening TT despite riding well indicates he cannot match Evenepoel's power-to-weight on TT climbs
rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: finished second fastest before favorites despite spring knee injury, suggesting UAE has unexpected depth for mountainous TTs
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: held provisional lead until final starters, confirming he climbs better in TT position than pure specialists like KÜNG
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: positive: fastest on the 2km climb segment despite losing 7 seconds on flat, revealing pure climbing form while sacrificing TT power
